page.title: Style engine
page.openInCreativeHub: Take this style to the Creative Hub

messages.fromBookAnalysis: Style "{{name}}" came from a book analysis — you can keep reviewing rules, run test writes, or bind it to a target.
messages.extractionTaskQueued: Style extraction task "{{title}}" was submitted. It runs in the background and auto-saves; the result will open automatically when done.
messages.extractionTaskMissing: The style extraction task is missing or has been cleaned up — please submit again.
messages.extractionTaskAutoSavedNoResult: The extraction task finished, but no auto-saved result came back.
messages.extractionTaskFailed: Style extraction task failed — please retry later.
messages.extractionTaskAutoSaved: Style "{{name}}" was auto-saved — the editor is open and ready.
messages.extractionTaskSubmittedNoDetail: Style extraction task was submitted, but no task details came back.
messages.manualCreated: Style "{{name}}" has been created — keep adding rules, run test writes, or bind it to a target.
messages.briefCreated: Style "{{name}}" has been generated — keep adding rules, run test writes, or bind it to a target.
messages.templateCreated: Template style "{{name}}" has been created — keep adding rules, run test writes, or bind it to a target.
messages.reextractWithFeatures: Re-extracted {{count}} features — confirm them and save.
messages.reextractEmpty: Still no usable features this round — check whether the source sample is complete enough.
messages.bindingCreated: This style will now participate in generation for the target.
messages.rewriteDone: Revised draft is ready — keep reviewing and tuning inside Strip AI flavor.

editorDialog.title: Edit current style
editorDialog.description: This window only manages the style's own settings. Apply, test, and Strip AI flavor each live in their own entry to avoid mixing them together.

workbenchDialog.title: Apply and test the current style
workbenchDialog.description: This window only handles binding to novels and chapters and running test writes — it does not change the style's own fields.

cleanDialog.title: Strip AI flavor
cleanDialog.description: This window only detects and revises prose. It does not edit style fields and does not mix in binding or test-write actions.

advancedWorkspace.intro: Now editing the settings for style "{{name}}". Apply, test, and Strip AI flavor are in their own entries — this view focuses on the style itself.
advancedWorkspace.introEmpty: No style is loaded for editing. Head back to the style list and select or create one.

bookStyle.title: Set the book-level default style from the novel info page
bookStyle.description: The style engine creates, tests, and organizes style assets. The default style for this novel is chosen on the novel info page, then carried into the director and writing flows.
bookStyle.entryWithNovel: The default style for this novel{{novelLabel}} now lives on the novel info page.
bookStyle.entryWithoutNovel: Open a novel's info page first to confirm its book-level default style.
bookStyle.splitNovelPage: Novel page: pick the default style for this novel, trigger recommendations, compare candidates, and decide when to feed it to the auto-director.
bookStyle.splitEngine: Style engine: keep organizing style assets, running test writes, stripping AI flavor, and managing rules.
bookStyle.nextDescription: Head to the novel page to confirm this book's default style. If the asset library doesn't have a good fit yet, come back to the style engine to create or refine one.
bookStyle.goToNovelPage: Set default style on the novel page

cleanFlow.title: Strip the AI flavor out of this draft
cleanFlow.description: Detection points out the passages that read like template copy, then a revised draft is generated for direct comparison. Recurring issues are summarized into rule suggestions so you can bring them back to the style editor.
cleanFlow.inputPlaceholder: Paste the passage you want to strip AI flavor from.
cleanFlow.issueEmpty: Run detection first — this panel will tell you which passages sound most like AI and why they pull readers out of the story.
cleanFlow.diffHint: A lightweight line-by-line comparison so you can quickly tell whether the revision flattens template feel or also strips the original voice.
cleanFlow.diffEmpty: Once you generate a revision, the before/after comparison will show up here.
cleanFlow.suggestionHeading: Recurring issues worth turning into rules
cleanFlow.suggestionHint: This pass won't force-write anything; it gathers recurring issues into suggestions so you can decide in the style editor whether to lock them in.
cleanFlow.suggestionEmpty: Once detection surfaces issues, rule suggestions will appear here.

cleanPanel.title: Strip AI flavor
cleanPanel.introWithProfile: Detecting and revising according to style "{{name}}". This panel only addresses AI flavor in the prose — it does not modify the style's own fields.
cleanPanel.introEmpty: Pick a style from the list first, then come back to detect prose.
cleanPanel.antiAiHint: If this style is bound to anti-AI rules, detection and rewriting will judge problems against those constraints first.
cleanPanel.antiAiEmpty: This style isn't bound to any explicit anti-AI rules. Detection will rely more on generic risk heuristics, so results may not match expectations.
cleanPanel.detectHint: Paste the prose you want to inspect. Submitting a complete scene at a time makes it easier to surface narration-, dialogue-, and exposition-voice issues.
cleanPanel.detectionEmpty: Detection results land here, calling out which sentences sound most like AI voice, why they read as risky, and which directions to try.

createDialog.title: Create style
createDialog.description: Pick the lightest starting path. After creation, the style editor opens automatically so you can keep adding rules, running test writes, and binding to targets.

quickStart.intro: When clear rules aren't ready yet, starting from a template is the lightest path. You can keep refining afterwards.

blank.intro: You can hand-build a blank style, or describe the reading feel you want and let the AI sketch the first skeleton.
blank.manualHint: Good when you already know what kind of style you want to maintain and just need a shell.
blank.aiTitle: Let AI sketch one for me
blank.aiHint: Good when you only know how you want the reading to feel and don't want to fill rule fields by hand yet.
blank.aiBriefPlaceholder: e.g. brisk overall pace, dense conflict, direct dialogue, no emotional dragging — suits urban and hot-blooded progression novels.

extract.intro: Create a style from source material. Direct text and knowledge-base documents go through a background extraction task; book analyses use their voice and craft sections directly.
extract.directTextPlaceholder: Paste the reference text you want the style extracted from. Ideally include at least one complete scene.
extract.knowledgeSearchPlaceholder: Search by knowledge document title or file name
extract.knowledgeNotFound: No knowledge documents available. Upload originals to the knowledge base first, then come back here to create a style.
extract.knowledgeMeta: Active version v{{version}} · {{versionCount}} versions · {{analysisCount}} book analyses
extract.knowledgeSampleHint: The style asset still keeps the full source preview; the model only reads representative samples to avoid feeding an entire long work in one shot.
extract.knowledgeFullTextWarning: Full-text extraction sends the entire active version to the model. For long works, prefer representative sampling first.
extract.knowledgePreviewLoading: Loading the selected document's active version...
extract.knowledgeMissingActive: The selected document has no active version and cannot be submitted.
extract.knowledgeEmptyContent: The active version's content is empty and cannot be submitted.
extract.knowledgePromptSelect: After you pick a knowledge document, the system reads the active version and freezes a snapshot when the task is submitted.
extract.bookAnalysisSearchPlaceholder: Search by analysis title or source document
extract.bookAnalysisNotFound: No available book analyses. Once you finish one, you can generate a style directly from here.
extract.bookAnalysisFallbackSummary: Book analysis ready for style generation

submit.knowledge: Extract from knowledge source and auto-save

bookAnalysisPanel.title: Generate from book analysis
bookAnalysisPanel.hint: This path reads the voice and craft sections of the analysis and opens the style editor right after generating.
bookAnalysisPanel.promptSelect: After picking a book analysis, the system uses its voice and craft sections to generate the style — no background task is involved.

presetPanel.title: Retention strategy
presetPanel.hint: Decide how much of the original fingerprint to keep first — the system applies this strategy when it auto-generates and saves.
presetPanel.presetSubmitHint: Submits with "{{label}}" to the background. Once it finishes, the style is auto-saved — no manual save needed.

task.title: Background task
task.noTaskHint: Once submitted, the system handles extraction, applies your retention strategy, and saves the style in the background. You can close this dialog — the result will open automatically when done.

imitate.label: Hew close to the source
imitate.summary: Retain strong fingerprints and expressive habits — best for short-term imitation, voice trials, and side-by-side study.

balanced.label: Keep the core reading feel
balanced.summary: Keep reading feel and pacing, soften overly strong fingerprints — works for most projects out of the box.

transfer.label: Only carry over reusable skeleton
transfer.summary: Retain reusable pacing, structure, and dialogue logic — best for long projects and book-wide defaults.

directText.label: Paste text directly
directText.summary: Hand the sample text to a background extraction task — great when you already have a passage or chapter.

knowledgeDocument.label: Knowledge document
knowledgeDocument.summary: Pick a knowledge document's active version — the system freezes the full-text snapshot and learns from representative samples.

bookAnalysis.label: Book analysis
bookAnalysis.summary: Use the voice and craft results inside a book analysis directly to generate a style.

representativeSample.label: Smart sampling
representativeSample.summary: Sample representative passages from the opening, middle, late, and closing sections — better for long works in both speed and stability.

fullText.label: Full-text extraction
fullText.summary: Feed the entire active version to the model — good for short docs; long works may be slower and more likely to hit context or timeout limits.

editorPanel.title: Edit current style
editorPanel.emptyPrompt: Head back to the style list, pick a style, then come here to edit.
editorPanel.starterNotice: This is one of the preset starter styles. Modify it for your project directly — no need to copy first.
editorPanel.primaryGuide: If you'd rather not face a wall of technical fields up front, focus on the four essentials first: style name, summary, applicable genres, and anti-AI rules. The four advanced rule blocks below are the system's finer-grain control layer — leave them alone if you're unsure.

basics.title: Basic positioning
basics.hint: State up front how this style should feel to read — this content is also surfaced first when the list expands.

name.label: Style name
name.hint: This is the main title you'll recognize this style by in the list — include the genre or reading feel where possible.

category.label: Category
category.hint: For your own filing — e.g. urban, fantasy, romance, fast-progression hot-blooded.

description.label: One-line summary
description.hint: One complete sentence describing the reading feel, pacing, or character voice this style should produce.
description.placeholder: e.g. dense conflict, fast pacing, direct dialogue, externalized emotion — suits urban progression novels.

tags.label: Tags
tags.hint: For your own retrieval — short keywords, comma-separated.
tags.placeholder: e.g. wish-fulfillment, fast pace, strong conflict

applicableGenres.label: Applicable genres
applicableGenres.hint: Tell the system which genres or scenarios this style fits — comma-separated.

source.title: Source evidence and extracted features
source.hint: This is the "evidence layer" of the style. Styles extracted from text or knowledge documents rely on this for review and re-extraction. Feature notes, evidence, scores, preset suggestions, and recommended rules all live here.

sourceContent.label: Source sample
sourceContent.hint: Keep the text used when this style was extracted. The more complete the sample, the more stable the features the system can pull.
sourceContent.placeholder: The source sample used when extracting this style asset

source.featuresHint: Style features pulled from the source are listed here. Checked means it stays in this style.
source.noExtractedFeatures: This text-based style has no selectable feature entries yet. Click "Re-extract features" to rebuild the feature pool from the source sample.
source.presetSuggestionHint: The model suggests three retention plans. The one currently saved is highlighted so you can decide whether to switch retention strength.
source.antiAiSuggestionTitle: Anti-AI rules suggested by the model
source.antiAiSuggestionHint: These rules were recommended for binding during extraction. Bound rules are highlighted; unbound ones still keep the original suggested name.

analysis.title: Analysis draft for the system
analysis.hint: This isn't reader-facing copy — it's a note for you and the system on later review. Explain why this style is worth keeping and its most important qualities.
analysis.placeholder: e.g. this style keeps strong pacing and direct dialogue front and center, not detailed lyricism.

rules.title: Advanced rule blocks
rules.hint: These four blocks are the machine-readable rule layer the system actually reads at runtime. When unsure about a field, read the title and hint first, then decide. If only the "summary" field shows up, the extraction stayed at summary level and fine-grained fields are still sparse.
rules.compatibilityNotice: Compatibility fields are mainly for legacy assets and limited experiments: {{fields}}. For stable reading feel, focus on the expression-level summaries and anti-AI rules.

narrative.title: Narrative progression rules
narrative.hint: Controls how the plot moves, how scenes close, multi-POV usage, and whether loop hooks are allowed.

character.title: Character expression rules
character.hint: Controls how characters speak, how emotion surfaces, whether self-reflection is encouraged, and whether saving face is prioritized.

language.title: Language texture rules
language.hint: Controls sentence roughness, colloquial level, sentence variation, and whether incomplete sentences are allowed.

rhythm.title: Rhythm and density rules
rhythm.hint: Controls pacing speed, paragraph density, and whether action takes priority over explanation.

card.empty: This rule block has no readable fields yet. Lean on the summary and anti-AI rules above; open the advanced JSON when fine compatibility is truly needed.
card.advancedHint: This keeps the raw JSON entry point — mainly for legacy asset compatibility or fine tuning. The readable fields above are usually enough.
card.parseError: The current JSON structure can't be parsed. The system will fall back to an empty object on save — fix the format first.

antiAi.title: Bind anti-AI rules
antiAi.hint: This decides which problems the system guards against during detection and revision. The clearer the binding, the more directed "Strip AI flavor" becomes.

editorPanel.saveFooter: After saving, the expanded details, Strip AI flavor detection, and apply/test all read the new settings.

imitateFlow.title: Imitate a style
imitateFlow.description: Give me a concrete reference passage and I'll break it into an executable style — then you decide whether to hug it tightly, keep the skeleton, or only carry over the reusable parts.
imitateFlow.namePlaceholder: Style name, e.g. Cool realist dialogue flow
imitateFlow.sourceTextPlaceholder: Paste the reference text you want to imitate. Include at least one full scene so the system can pick up narrative distance, dialogue feel, and sentence rhythm.
imitateFlow.presetHint: `imitate` suits short imitation exercises, `balanced` works for most projects, and `transfer` is best for book-wide binding — it actively avoids high-fingerprint risk.
imitateFlow.presetEmpty: Finish feature extraction first — the three retention strategies and their reading-feel notes will appear here.
imitateFlow.step3: 3. Preview the style skeleton about to be saved
imitateFlow.step3Hint: After saving, the style editor opens automatically — keep tuning rules, bind it to a target, or take it straight into a test write.
imitateFlow.previewEmpty: No features to preview yet. Finish feature extraction first, then decide how to save.

landing.heroTitle: Pick a style first, then decide whether to edit, apply, or strip AI flavor.
landing.heroDescription: The home page surfaces every style asset you have. Expanding a card shows its reading feel, rule summary, anti-AI constraints, and current maturity.
landing.bookHint: Book-level defaults live on the novel info page — the novel itself picks the asset to use, then carries it into the director and writing flows.
landing.emptyDescription: Create the first style now — you can come back later to add rules, run test writes, and bind targets.
landing.customHint: These are the reusable assets you've shaped — pick from here first.
landing.starterHint: These preset assets are good for borrowing a skeleton, then reshaping into your own project's style.

detail.readingFeelHint: This column lets you quickly judge what this style is going for and which projects it's best for first.
detail.rulesHint: These are the four rule layers that actually drive the reading feel — easy to understand from the list directly.
detail.antiAiHint: This decides which risks the system watches most closely during detection and revision.
detail.extractionAntiAiSuggestion: Extraction additionally suggested {{count}} anti-AI rules — good for further fine-tuning.
detail.antiAiEmpty: This style has no explicit anti-AI constraints bound, so "Strip AI flavor" will be less directional.
detail.overviewHint: This column mainly helps you gauge how mature this style is right now.
detail.noBoundNovel: Not bound to a novel yet
detail.nextStepHint: Each button now owns a single job — they no longer jump into the same panel.
detail.nextEdit: Edit settings: maintain the style's own notes, rules, and anti-AI constraints.
detail.nextWorkbench: Apply & test: bind to a novel or chapter and run a test write.
detail.nextClean: Strip AI flavor: only detection and revision — no style field changes.

rulesPanel.title: Anti-AI rules
rulesPanel.intro: View, create, and tune anti-AI rules in the Rules Center. The style editor decides which rules bind to the current style.

sidebar.pickFirstTitle: Pick a style first, then fine-tune
sidebar.pickFirstIntro: On your first run, don't try to understand every rule field. Pick a preset that feels closest to what you want, then rename, retag, and adjust rules — it's much smoother.
sidebar.editableHint: Including {{count}} presets — copy the idea, then refine.
sidebar.templatesHint: Great for quickly spinning up a new style instead of starting from scratch.
sidebar.switchHint: This panel only switches the edit target in the dialog. The full asset list is on the home page.
sidebar.emptyPrompt: No style assets yet. Click "Create or import style" above and spin one up from a template — the lightest path.

dialog.title: Create or import style
dialog.description: Start with "Quick start" or the sentence-based generator in "Blank / AI". Use "Extract from text" once you have stable sample text in hand.

quickStart.intro: A few editable starter styles are over on the left. To spin up another, generating from a template is lighter — then tune it for your project.

blank.intro: Two light ways to start: build a blank manually if you know which rules to maintain, or describe the feel you want and let AI sketch the skeleton.
blank.manualHint: Good when you know which style rules to maintain and just want a shell to fill in.
blank.manualNamePlaceholder: e.g. My female-lead urban relationship style
blank.aiTitle: Let AI sketch one for me
blank.aiHint: When you'd rather not study rule fields yet, describe the reading feel, character, or references — AI will sketch an editable style.
blank.aiNamePlaceholder: Style name (optional — leave blank and AI names it)
blank.aiBriefPlaceholder: e.g. similar to "The Distant Savior" — restrained overall, philosophical, dialogue with edge, less inspirational pep talk, more real-world friction.

extract.intro: For when you have stable reference text and want the system to extract features before editing. Without a ready sample, start from a template or AI first.
extract.submit: Have AI extract features and create

workbench.title: Apply and test the current style
workbench.intro: This panel only handles two things: binding this style to a novel/chapter/task and running a test write to check the result. "Strip AI flavor" lives in its own entry now and is no longer mixed in here.
workbench.bindingHint: Once bound, this style participates in generation for the matching novel, chapter, or task. Higher priority means earlier influence; higher weight means stronger participation.
workbench.bindingsEmpty: This style isn't bound to any target yet. Bind it to a novel or chapter first so downstream generation will pick it up automatically.
workbench.testWriteHint: When you're unsure whether the style has truly landed, generating or rewriting a passage is the most direct check.
workbench.topicPlaceholder: e.g. the protagonist's first public comeback
workbench.rewritePlaceholder: Paste the prose you want this style to rewrite
workbench.testWriteEmpty: Test-write output lands here. Use it to judge whether the style's pacing, dialogue feel, and overall voice are in place.

rulePresentation.narrativeEmpty: No explicit narrative progression summary yet.
rulePresentation.characterEmpty: No explicit character expression summary yet.
rulePresentation.languageEmpty: No explicit language texture summary yet.

emotionExpression.light_behavior: Surfaces through light action and reaction

multiPov.false: Try to stay in a single POV

landingItems.readingFeelFallback: This style hasn't spelled out its reading-feel positioning yet.
